title: Key lime pie
subtitle: Digestive biscuit base with a sharp lime custard
excerpt: A no-cook filling of condensed milk, egg yolks, and fresh lime juice set in a biscuit crumb base. The acid in the lime juice reacts with the condensed milk to set the filling without heat; a short bake finishes it firm. Sharp, rich, and simple.
sourceNotes: Key lime pie originates in the Florida Keys, where Key limes (Citrus aurantifolia) are native. The earliest printed recipes date from the late 19th and early 20th centuries, associated with the condensed milk introduced to the region by John Gail Borden. The condensed milk and egg yolk filling that sets without cooking was documented before 1930, when it entered the public domain. The original uses a pastry shell; the digestive biscuit base is a later British adaptation. Key limes are replaced here with standard Persian limes, which are widely available.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make up to 2 days ahead and keep refrigerated. Add cream just before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven. Fan at 130°C. Low heat sets the custard without curdling the egg yolks.

glossaryTerms.definition: The filling for key lime pie sets because the citric acid in fresh lime juice reacts with the proteins in condensed milk, partially denaturing them and thickening the mixture before it goes into the oven. The bake finishes the setting process by cooking the egg yolks. This is why bottled lime juice does not work as well as fresh: it has lower acidity.

content.text: Key lime pie has a filling that requires almost no effort and sets reliably. The
thickens on contact with lime juice and firms completely after 15 minutes in a low oven. Use full-fat condensed milk and freshly squeezed limes; the sharpness is the point.

Preheat the oven to 150°C conventional. Crush the digestive biscuits to fine crumbs. Stir together with the melted butter and sugar until the crumbs hold when pressed. Press firmly and evenly into the base and sides of a 20 cm tart tin. Bake for 10 minutes until set and lightly coloured. Leave to cool.

Whisk together the condensed milk, egg yolks, lime juice, and lime zest until smooth and slightly thickened. Pour into the cooled base. Bake for 15 minutes until just set with a slight wobble in the centre. Remove and leave to cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving. Serve with whipped double cream.
